differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (secondary to SGLT2 inhibitor therapy)
High anion gap metabolic acidosis due to 5-oxoprolinemia (pyroglutamic acidosis)
Acute kidney injury with uremic acidosis
Type B lactic acidosis (due to underlying malignancy)
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas analysis to calculate the anion gap and confirm the degree of acidemia
Measure serum lactate, ketones (beta-hydroxybutyrate), and salicylate levels
Order serum electrolyte panel including glucose, creatinine, and calculated anion gap
Evaluate for occult infection or worsening heart failure as precipitating factors
Consider urine organic acid testing if initial workup is inconclusive to rule out 5-oxoproline

differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (EDKA) secondary to SGLT2 inhibitor (dapagliflozin)
Acute hypercapnic respiratory failure (carbon dioxide narcosis)
High anion gap metabolic acidosis due to 5-oxoprolinemia (pyroglutamic acidosis)
next_steps: Measure arterial blood gas (ABG) for pH, bicarbonate, pCO2, and anion gap calculation
Obtain serum beta-hydroxybutyrate, lactate, and electrolytes
Obtain serum morning cortisol and ACTH levels to rule out adrenal insufficiency
Hold all SGLT2 inhibitors and metformin permanently
Monitor respiratory status closely with serial blood gases and ensure strict adherence to non-invasive ventilation (biPAP)
Perform imaging (CT of abdomen/pelvis with contrast or renal ultrasound) to evaluate for obstructive uropathy or pyelonephritis
Perform comprehensive medication review and toxicology screening (serum salicylate and acetaminophen levels)
